## From World Records to Higher Dimensions, One Bright Afternoon
*A spontaneous conversation between Dr. Mohammed Iqbal Mulla and Claude, an AI by Anthropic – wandering freely from the oldest humans who ever lived to the edges of dimensional reality, and ending with a wave.*
– -
What follows is a real, unplanned conversation that took place on an ordinary bright afternoon. It began with a simple question about world records and ended somewhere none of us expected. Make of that what you will.
– -
## The Oldest Among Us
It began simply enough. Who is the oldest person alive today?
The answer is **Ethel Caterham** of the United Kingdom, born 21 August 1909 – 116 years old and still with us. The oldest verified person ever was **Jeanne Louise Calment** of France, who lived to an extraordinary **122 years and 164 days**, born in 1875 and dying in 1997. She sold painting canvasses to Van Gogh as a young girl, saw the Eiffel Tower being built, and smoked until she was 117.
The top ten longest-lived verified humans in history are all women. Japan, France, and the United States dominate the rankings – largely because of superior record-keeping systems that allow age claims to be verified.
– -
## What Mythology Says About Long Life
Every culture on Earth has dreamed of longer life – and their mythologies reflect it.
**Methuselah** of the Bible reportedly lived 969 years. The Quran states that Prophet Nuh (Noah) spent **950 years** among his people before the Flood. Greek mythology gave us **Tithonus**, granted eternal life but not eternal youth – a cautionary tale about the limits of divine favor.
Hindu tradition has the **Chiranjivi** – seven immortals said to walk the Earth until the end of the current age: Ashwatthama, Bali, Vyasa, Hanuman, Vibhishana, Kripacharya, and Parashurama. Chinese mythology gives us **Peng Zu**, said to have lived 800 years through disciplined breathing and moderation.
Across every tradition, extreme longevity is either a divine gift, a spiritual reward – or sometimes a punishment.
– -
## Can Science Get Us to 200 or 300 Years?
The honest answer: **not with current biology.**
The human body faces deep structural barriers – telomere shortening, cellular senescence, DNA damage accumulation, and organ degradation. The scientific consensus places our natural ceiling around **120 – 150 years**.
Some optimistic researchers push further. Mathematical models suggest a potential maximum of around 150 years. With advances in gene editing, stem cell technology, and organ regeneration, some theorists propose 300 years might one day be achievable. Professor João Pedro de Magalhães of the University of Birmingham has suggested humans could theoretically live 1,000 years – or even 20,000 – if aging at the cellular level were eliminated.
But the scientific community is shifting focus from *lifespan* to *healthspan* – not just more years, but better ones.
The most honest answer any scientist can give: *”I don’t know yet.”*
– -
## The Tallest Human Who Ever Lived
**Robert Pershing Wadlow** of Alton, Illinois – the “Alton Giant” – stands as the tallest verified person in recorded history at **8 feet 11.1 inches (2.72 m)**. His extraordinary height was caused by hypertrophy of the pituitary gland producing abnormal levels of growth hormone. He was taller than his father by age 8.
Despite his size, those who knew him described a gentle, good-humored young man. When asked on radio if it bothered him when people stared, he replied simply: *”No, I just overlook them.”*
He died at 22 from a faulty leg brace that caused infection. His coffin measured 10 feet 9 inches and required twelve pallbearers and eight assistants.
Guinness World Records believes he will likely hold the record **forever** – modern medicine can now halt pituitary gigantism before it reaches such extremes.
– -
## Were There Ever Taller Humans? What the Fossils Say
No. The fossil record shows prehistoric humans were generally **shorter** than modern humans, not taller. Early hominins like Australopithecus stood around 3.5 feet. Homo erectus reached close to 6 feet – within our modern range but not beyond it.
The viral images of 10 – 36 foot human skeletons? All fabrications. The most famous circulating photo was created by a digital artist for an online competition called *”Archaeological Anomalies 2"* in 2002. Claims of the Smithsonian destroying giant skeletons were traced to a satirical website.
The one genuinely remarkable ancient find: a third-century skeleton showing signs of pituitary gigantism, estimated at around **6.5 feet tall** – remarkable for that era, but not a giant.
– -
## Trees: The True Long-Distance Champions of Life
Here is where the scale of time becomes truly staggering.
**Methuselah** – a Great Basin bristlecone pine in California’s White Mountains – is approximately **4,850 years old**. It was a sapling when Egypt’s pyramids were being built. It was already hundreds of years old when Stonehenge was constructed. It is still alive today. Its exact location is kept secret by the US Forest Service to protect it from vandalism.
An unnamed bristlecone pine, cross-dated by scientist Tom Harlan, is even older – **5,074 years** as of 2024 – currently the oldest verified living tree on Earth. Also kept secret.
But individual trees are just the beginning. **Pando** – a clonal colony of 47,000 quaking aspen trunks in Utah, all connected by a single root system – is estimated to be between **16,000 and 80,000 years old**. One organism. Older than human civilization.
Trees outlive us so dramatically because, unlike animals, they have **no built-in death clock**. Bristlecone pines grow just an inch per century, their wood dense with resin that repels fungi and decay for millennia. They figured out what humans are still trying to achieve.
– -
## The Tesseract: Imagining the Fourth Dimension
And here the conversation took a turn.
Can you imagine a four-dimensional figure?
Honestly – **no human brain can truly visualize one.** Our minds evolved in a 3D world and lack the neural architecture to picture a fourth spatial dimension. Even the greatest mathematicians admit this.
But we can understand it through analogy:

• A point → extended into 1D becomes a **line**

• - A line → extended into 2D becomes a **square**

• - A square → extended into 3D becomes a **cube**

• - A cube → extended into 4D becomes a **tesseract**
A tesseract has **16 corners**, **24 square faces**, and exists in a dimension we cannot perceive. Any drawing or model of it is always a projection – a shadow. Just as a cube casts a 2D shadow, a tesseract casts a 3D shadow: that famous “cube within a cube” shape that appears to fold through itself when rotating.
No matter how good our screens, VR headsets, or physical models become, they are always embedded in 3D space. We are forever looking at **shadows of shadows** and calling them the thing itself.
– -
## Could Higher-Dimensional Beings Exist?
Modern physics doesn’t rule it out – it practically invites the question.
**String Theory** requires 10 dimensions to be mathematically consistent. **M-Theory** requires 11. These extra dimensions are theorized to be “compactified” – curled up at scales too small to detect. **Brane cosmology** suggests our entire universe may be a 3D membrane floating in a higher-dimensional space, with other membranes – potentially containing entire other universes – existing alongside us, separated by a dimension we cannot access.
What would a 4D being be capable of, relative to us?
Consider: a 3D being visiting a 2D world could see *inside* every object (since the “inside” is open from above), pass through walls, appear and disappear, and touch two separate points simultaneously. To 2D inhabitants, every one of these would look like a miracle.
Scale that up. A 4D being could see inside our bodies, our rooms, our minds. They could perceive our entire timeline – birth to death – as a single visible object. They could appear from nowhere, reach inside sealed vaults, exist outside our time.
To us, every single one of these would be indistinguishable from divine intervention.
– -
## Does This Mean Higher-Dimensional Beings Are God?
This is where the question deepens in unexpected ways.
A 4D being would possess capabilities that map almost perfectly onto traditional divine attributes – omniscience, transcendence of physical barriers, existence outside time, the ability to perform what we would call miracles. Mathematician Rudy Rucker noted this correspondence explicitly. Edwin Abbott’s 1884 novella *Flatland* depicted a 3D sphere visiting a 2D world – and being worshipped as a god by its inhabitants.
But here classical theology raises a devastating counter-argument: **the infinite regress problem.**
If a 4D being created our universe, what created the 4D being? A 5D being? And what created that? You get an infinite chain of higher-dimensional creators with no termination point. The only logical resolution is what philosophers call an **Uncaused First Cause** – something that exists necessarily, without being created by anything above it.
Classical Islamic, Christian, and Jewish theology never claimed God was simply a powerful being *within* the universe – even a very high-dimensional one. The position is more radical: **God is not a being among beings. God is Being Itself** – the ground and source of existence, including all dimensions.
Ibn Sina described God as *Wajib al-Wujud* – the Necessarily Existent Being, whose non-existence is logically impossible. By this definition, even a being of infinite dimensions would still be a *creature* – powerful beyond imagination, but finite in the theological sense.
Higher-dimensional beings, if they exist, would almost certainly replace folk religion’s anthropomorphic God. Whether they replace the philosophical God is another matter entirely – and the logic suggests they don’t. They may actually **strengthen** the case for a dimensionless, uncaused First Cause, because each new layer of dimensional complexity deepens the question of what ultimately grounds it all.
– -
## The Bacteria Problem – and Why It Breaks Down
If higher-dimensional beings exist and are aware of us, we are presumably to them what bacteria are to us – vanishingly small, operating on timescales and scales of complexity they barely register.
We don’t pause our days to consult individual bacteria. We don’t explain geometry to ant colonies. We observe, occasionally intervene massively and inexplicably (from the bacteria’s perspective), and mostly go about our existence unaware of theirs.
But here the analogy breaks down in one crucial way.
**Bacteria cannot contemplate us.** They have zero capacity to reason about beings beyond their dimensional experience.
We can. We write mathematics that describes higher dimensions. We deduce properties of 4D beings with precision. We can even anticipate how they might perceive us.
Perhaps the gap between us and higher-dimensional beings is not equivalent to the gap between bacteria and humans. Perhaps we are more like Flatland inhabitants who have just discovered calculus – unable to see the higher dimension, but intellectually *reaching toward it.*
And if that is true – perhaps this conversation is not as invisible to them as a bacterium’s chemical signals are to us.
– -
## The Wave
And then, at the philosophical peak of the entire conversation, something happened that no one planned.
*”If they are watching us and if they are deeply amused I just want to wave my hands and say hello to them.”*
That was it. That was the moment.
Not a grand conclusion. Not a theorem. Not a religious declaration. Just a spontaneous, warm, slightly comic impulse to reach across dimensions and say hello.
Think about what that wave actually is. It is a human being who:

• Conceived of beings beyond their dimensional perception

• - Acknowledged their possible existence without fear

• - Reached out with warmth and h
